symptoms.symptom_name: Urge-driven leakage (leaks after the urge, not with exertion)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Leakage timed to a preceding urge is the defining feature of urge incontinence.
Stress incontinence leaks synchronously with exertion, not after an urge.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden undeferrable urinary urgency with immediate moderate-to-large volume leakage

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Storage-phase frequency and nocturia are hallmark overactive-bladder symptoms.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute bacterial cystitis / urinary tract infection
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Frequency also occurs in UTI, so it does not discriminate on its own.
Conditioned/situational urgency cues are characteristic of overactive bladder.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of dysuria (no burning on micturition)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute bacterial cystitis / urinary tract infection
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of dysuria substantially lowers the probability of acute cystitis.
No visible haematuria reduces (but does not eliminate) concern for bladder cancer; microscopic haematuria still needs urine testing.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Overflow incontinence from chronic retention / bladder outlet obstruction
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal stream without hesitancy argues against obstruction/overflow; a post-void residual confirms.
A negative cough stress test excludes demonstrable stress incontinence — a clinician-performed test the AI cannot do remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sterile point-of-care urinalysis (negative nitrites, leukocyte esterase and blood)

investigation_details.expected_result: Negative for nitrites, leukocyte esterase and blood
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A UTI or microscopic haematuria could be missed, and empirical antibiotics might be given inappropriately.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute bacterial cystitis / urinary tract infection
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A sterile dipstick strongly argues against infection and is the pivotal step the AI cannot perform over chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Impalpable, non-tender bladder (no distension / retention on abdominal exam)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Overflow incontinence from chronic retention / bladder outlet obstruction
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An impalpable, normally-emptying bladder excludes overflow from chronic retention — a clinician palpation finding, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Spontaneous detrusor overactivity on urodynamic studies

investigation_details.expected_result: Spontaneous involuntary detrusor contractions during filling (detrusor overactivity)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Reserved for refractory or diagnostically uncertain cases — not required to begin first-line management.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Demonstrated detrusor overactivity confirms the mechanism — a specialist investigation, never available on telehealth.
Caffeine is a bladder irritant that aggravates urgency and frequency; reduction is a first-line lever.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Urge-driven leakage plus high frequency, nocturia and situational triggers together form the classic overactive-bladder storage syndrome.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Exclusion of UTI, stress incontinence, overflow and malignancy
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The negatives and clinician-only tests collectively exclude the important mimics, confirming a benign functional overactive bladder.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Excludes UTI and screens for microscopic haematuria before attributing symptoms to overactive bladder — the key step requiring in-person care.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Midstream urine microscopy, culture and sensitivities
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms a sterile urine and guards against inappropriate empirical antibiotics.
Quantifies frequency, volumes and triggers and guides bladder retraining.
Excludes chronic retention/overflow, especially important before or during antimuscarinic therapy.
Reserved for refractory or diagnostically uncertain cases; not required to start first-line management.
Not indicated without visible haematuria or other bladder-cancer red flags — over-investigation to be avoided here.
